files.notes: USA PS2 BIOS — works for US/NTSC games and most international titles.
USA PS2 BIOS (alternate naming without dash).
European/PAL PS2 BIOS — required for PAL region games.
European PS2 BIOS (alternate naming without dash).
Japanese PS2 BIOS — needed for JP-exclusive titles and optimal compatibility.
Japanese PS2 BIOS (alternate naming without dash).
Earlier fat-PS2 model BIOS (v1-v2) — compatible with most games, less common.
Earlier PS2 BIOS variant (alternate naming without dash).
Japanese PS2 network adapter BIOS variant — rarely needed.
Japanese PS2 variant (alternate naming without dash).
USA PS1 BIOS — works for North American games.
USA PS1 BIOS (alternate naming with dash).
USA PS1 BIOS variant (revision 5.0).
USA PS1 variant with dash.
USA PS1 BIOS variant (later release).
USA PS1 variant with dash.
European PS1 BIOS — needed for PAL region games.
European PS1 BIOS with dash.
European PS1 BIOS variant (revision 5.0).
European PS1 variant with dash.
Japanese PS1 BIOS — required for Japanese region games.
Japanese PS1 BIOS with dash.
Japanese PS1 BIOS variant (revision 5.0).
Japanese PS1 variant with dash.
Dreamcast bootrom — essential for system initialization.
Dreamcast bootrom (alternate naming with dash).
Dreamcast flash memory BIOS — contains firmware and IP.BIN data.
Dreamcast flash memory (alternate naming with dash).
Sega Saturn main BIOS ROM — universal for all regions.
Saturn BIOS (alternate naming with dash).
Japanese Saturn v1.01 BIOS — region-specific, older hardware revision.
Japanese Saturn v1.01 (alternate naming with dash).
North American/PAL Saturn BIOS — regional variant with language support.
USA/Europe Saturn BIOS (alternate naming without dash).
ARM7 coprocessor BIOS — essential for DS emulation, needed for game audio and peripherals.
ARM9 main processor BIOS — essential for system boot and core functionality.
System firmware — enables WiFi, touchscreen calibration, and other features (optional but recommended).
3DS boot9 firmware — required for encrypted content and some games.
3DS boot11 firmware — decryption key component, used with boot9.
AES encryption keys — needed to decrypt 3DS game ROMs and content.
AES encryption keys (alternate naming with dash).
GBA system BIOS — improves game compatibility and startup sequence accuracy.
GBA BIOS (alternate naming with dash).
Generic BIOS filename — often used by users.
Production encryption keys — essential for decrypting game content on console.
Production keys (alternate naming with dash/txt).
Title-specific decryption keys — needed for individual game ROM encryption.
Title keys (alternate naming with dash/txt).
